versions.headline: Extended Support Release — enhanced compliance and audit logging
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release. Requires PostgreSQL 10+ or MySQL 5.7.12+. First ESR for teams needing long-term stability on the 7.x series.

versions.headline: Extended Support Release — performance and security improvements
breaking_changes: ServiceSettings.ListenAddress deprecated — use ServiceSettings.ListenAddress with explicit host binding.
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release. Requires PostgreSQL 10+ or MySQL 5.7.12+. Includes RBAC improvements and better plugin management. Last ESR before 8.x series.

versions.headline: Extended Support Release — Calls bundled, new boards features
breaking_changes: MySQL minimum raised to 8.0 — MySQL 5.7 is no longer supported.
Calls plugin is now bundled — remove standalone Calls plugin before upgrading.
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release. IMPORTANT: MySQL 5.7 is no longer supported — upgrade to MySQL 8.0+ before upgrading Mattermost. The Calls plugin is now bundled into the server — remove any standalone Calls plugin installation before upgrading to avoid conflicts. PostgreSQL 11+ required.

versions.headline: Improved performance for large-team message loading
Plugin management and notification improvements
Advanced access control and API improvements
Mobile push notification reliability and team management
Extended Support Release — enhanced compliance and audit logging
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release — officially supported for 9 months. Includes structured JSON audit log, compliance export improvements, and enhanced session security.

versions.headline: Channel bookmarks and improved webhook management
Post edit history and improved file sharing
File sharing improvements and scheduled messages
versions.upgrade_notes: TeamSettings.EnableOpenServer is deprecated in this release. Update your config to use TeamSettings.EnableUserCreation instead.

versions.headline: Enhanced search and thread improvements
Security hardening and CSRF protection upgrades
breaking_changes: Integrations not sending X-Requested-With header to state-changing endpoints will receive 403 Forbidden.
versions.upgrade_notes: IMPORTANT: All integrations must now send the X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest header on all state-changing API calls. Integrations missing this header will receive 403 Forbidden. Update all custom integrations before upgrading.

versions.headline: Final 9.x Extended Support Release — security hardening
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release. Last ESR in the 9.x series. TLS 1.3 enforcement option added. Improved SAML/LDAP diagnostic tools. Upgrade to 10.5 ESR when ready to move to 10.x.

versions.headline: Major release — AI Copilot, redesigned admin UI, PostgreSQL 13 minimum
breaking_changes: PostgreSQL minimum version raised to 13 — PostgreSQL 12 will not start.
MySQL minimum version raised to 8.0 — MySQL 5.7 no longer supported.
Legacy sidebar permanently removed — custom integrations using legacy sidebar API will break.
FileSettings.AmazonS3SSL renamed to FileSettings.AmazonS3SSLVerification — old key silently ignored.
versions.upgrade_notes: MAJOR RELEASE. CRITICAL: PostgreSQL 12 is no longer supported — upgrade your database to PostgreSQL 13+ before upgrading Mattermost. MySQL 5.7 is also no longer supported — upgrade to MySQL 8.0+. The legacy channel sidebar API is permanently removed. Check all custom integrations for sidebar API usage. Rename FileSettings.AmazonS3SSL to FileSettings.AmazonS3SSLVerification in your config.

versions.headline: AI enhancements and improved Calls plugin
versions.upgrade_notes: If using Elasticsearch: after upgrading, purge and re-index to resolve index mapping mismatch. System Console → Elasticsearch → Purge Indexes, then Index Now.

versions.headline: Compliance exports and admin delegation improvements
breaking_changes: LogSettings.FileLevel deprecated — use LogSettings.ConsoleLevel and advanced logging config.
versions.upgrade_notes: LogSettings.FileLevel is deprecated. Update your logging config to use LogSettings.ConsoleLevel and advanced logging configuration.

versions.headline: Stability and performance improvements
API improvements and Playbooks enhancements
Extended Support Release — security hardening and compliance improvements
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release. OAuth 2.0 PKCE support added for public clients. Personal Access Token rotation support. LDAP nested group sync (up to 3 levels). AI Copilot meeting notes generation. PostgreSQL 13+ and MySQL 8.0+ still required.

versions.headline: Collaboration improvements and custom status enhancements
Performance and Calls plugin improvements
AI improvements, accessibility and admin tooling

Feature release
Feature release
Extended Support Release — first 11.x ESR
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release. First ESR in the 11.x series. Recommended upgrade target from 10.5 ESR.

versions.headline: Feature release
Feature release

Feature release
Extended Support Release — latest ESR
versions.upgrade_notes: ESR release. Latest supported ESR as of June 2026. Recommended upgrade target for production environments.

versions.headline: Feature release
versions.upgrade_notes: WARNING: Versions 11.7.0, 11.7.1, and 11.7.2 contain a known database migration bug when upgrading from ≥10.11.17. Skip these versions and upgrade directly to 11.7.3 or later.

versions.headline: Patch release
versions.upgrade_notes: WARNING: Contains a known database migration bug when upgrading from ≥10.11.17. Skip this version and upgrade directly to 11.7.3 or later.

versions.headline: Patch release
versions.upgrade_notes: WARNING: Contains a known database migration bug when upgrading from ≥10.11.17. Skip this version and upgrade directly to 11.7.3 or later.

versions.headline: Patch release — fixes database migration bug
versions.upgrade_notes: Fixes the database migration bug present in 11.7.0–11.7.2 when upgrading from ≥10.11.17. Safe to upgrade to from any version.

known_bugs.title: Database migration bug: upgrading from ≥10.11.17 through 11.7.0–11.7.2
known_bugs.description: A database migration step is applied incorrectly when the source version is ≥10.11.17 and the target is any version from 11.7.0 to 11.7.2 (inclusive). This leaves the schema in a partially migrated state that causes runtime errors after startup.
known_bugs.workaround: Skip all 11.7.x versions below 11.7.3. If your current version is ≥10.11.17, upgrade directly to 11.7.3 or later.

known_bugs.title: CSRF header change breaks integrations on upgrade to 9.10+
known_bugs.description: From 9.10 onwards, all state-changing API calls require the X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest header. Integrations missing this header receive 403 Forbidden. This is a permanent breaking change — there is no config toggle to revert it.
known_bugs.workaround: Update all custom integrations and third-party tools to include the X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest header on every API request.

known_bugs.title: PostgreSQL 12 dropped in 10.0 — server will not start
known_bugs.description: Mattermost 10.0 requires PostgreSQL 13 as a minimum. Instances running PostgreSQL 12 will fail to start after upgrading to 10.0+. MySQL 5.7 is also dropped; MySQL 8.0 is the new minimum.
known_bugs.workaround: Upgrade your database to PostgreSQL 13+ (or MySQL 8.0+) BEFORE upgrading Mattermost. Do not upgrade Mattermost and the database simultaneously.

upgrade_notes_by_version.9.8: TeamSettings.EnableOpenServer is deprecated — replace with TeamSettings.EnableUserCreation in your config.
upgrade_notes_by_version.9.10: CSRF enforcement tightened: all integrations must now send X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest on state-changing API calls or receive 403. Update all custom integrations before upgrading.
upgrade_notes_by_version.10.0: MAJOR RELEASE. PostgreSQL 13+ required (12 dropped). MySQL 8.0+ required (5.7 dropped). Legacy sidebar API removed. Rename FileSettings.AmazonS3SSL → FileSettings.AmazonS3SSLVerification.
upgrade_notes_by_version.10.1: If using Elasticsearch/OpenSearch: purge and re-index after upgrade (index mapping changed). System Console → Elasticsearch → Purge Indexes → Index Now.
upgrade_notes_by_version.10.2: LogSettings.FileLevel deprecated — migrate to LogSettings.ConsoleLevel and advanced logging config.
upgrade_notes_by_version.11.7: Versions 11.7.0–11.7.2 contain a database migration bug for upgrades from ≥10.11.17. Upgrade directly to 11.7.3 or later.
